html:
Today, the world order faces heightened security tensions as Russia launched its biggest missile and drone strikes on Kyiv in weeks, killing at least 13 and hitting residential buildings, while researchers revealed an 18-month campaign of Russian drone surveillance over European nuclear sites.

Russia-Ukraine: Large-scale Russian attacks on Ukraine’s capital killed over a dozen people and injured scores, with casualties including children. These strikes coincide with Russia grappling with a deepening domestic fuel crisis due to Ukrainian long-range strikes against its oil refineries. German prosecutors, meanwhile, have charged a Ukrainian suspect over the Nord Stream explosions. Analysis by the International Institute of Strategic Studies (IISS) concluded Russia operated with "substantial impunity" in 144 drone surveillance incidents over nuclear sites in the UK, France, Belgium, and the Netherlands.

US-Iran Diplomacy: Indirect talks between the US and Iran in Qatar concluded with "positive progress," reported host Qatar and Trump, who said the US is "getting along well with Iran." These diplomatic movements follow Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death. Iran’s Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf called for a massive funeral turnout to "avenge the supreme leader's death in US-Israeli strikes," and rejected claims of IAEA access to bombed nuclear sites. A senior Chinese lawmaker will attend Khamenei’s funeral.

Great Power & US Politics: China continues to reshape the global space race, expanding its ambitions and positioning itself as a rival to the United States. Its new ethnic unity law has drawn international criticism, denounced as "forced assimilation" by rights groups. OpenAI is reportedly in early talks to give a 5% stake to the US government, a move CEO Sam Altman argued would share the benefits of AI. Donald Trump’s $2.2bn income last year is seen by historians as unprecedented, blurring conflict of interest lines. His relationship with Europe remains strained, with leaders reportedly closing ranks against his "barbs and pressure."

Global Economy & Security: Russia’s domestic fuel crisis and the long-term drone surveillance of European nuclear sites by Russia underscore broader security concerns. Alibaba will pay $600 million to settle allegations of illegal drug sales in a US probe, admitting failure to prevent 80,000 sales. Oman has outlined a plan for shipping companies to pay service fees to transit the Strait of Hormuz, pointing to Southeast Asian waterways as a blueprint.

Humanitarian & Regional Concerns: Venezuela is grappling with widespread suffering a week after deadly twin earthquakes, with untold casualties and growing humanitarian needs despite a low official death toll. In Gaza, an estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire due to "agonising delays for evacuation," according to the Hamas-run health ministry. In Sudan, Amnesty International accuses the Rapid Support Forces of committing ethnic cleansing and crimes against humanity in el-Fasher.
